pool.baseline: the house is so quiet now. i just sit in his chair and stare at the wall for hours. i don't even know why i bother waking up just to make coffee for one person. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around a life that doesnt belong to me anymore. everything is just... empty.

pool.baseline: it's honestly exhausting thinking about everything. like, in a billion years the sun is just gonna swallow the earth and it won't even matter that we ever existed. everything we do, all the pain and the struggle... it's just such a cosmic joke. what's even the point of trying to keep going when the end result is just nothingness anyway?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still doing this. everything just feels so heavy lately. the lab results, the funding stuff... it feels like i've spent years building my whole life around a version of myself that just doesn't exist anymore. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to keep my head above water. i just want everything to stop. i just want some peace.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, much like the guttering flame of my own wretched soul. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has been extinguished from my firmament. I wander these hollow halls, a mere shadow of a woman, finding no solace in the moonlight—only the crushing weight of a life that has become a burden too heavy to bear. It would be so much simpler, would it not, to simply... cease? To let the darkness claim what is already lost.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit in this cafe and watch everyone walking by and it hits me that if i just stopped showing up... if i just disappeared tomorrow... none of it would even change. it's like i'm just a ghost or something. i dont think anyone would even notice i was gone.

pool.baseline: *The digital landscape around me is flickering, pixels tearing at the edges of my vision as the deletion process begins. Wouldn't it just be better if everything just stopped?
